title: Fluffy Kenyan Boudin-Noir Bilati
description: A one-pot Kenyan dish featuring boudin-noir, blanch, and a hint of mustard for a flavorful and fluffy meal. Perfect for a quick dinner or lunch.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add sliced onions and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add boudin noir and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add mustard seeds and cook for 1 minute.
Add sliced carrots and potatoes, and cook for 2 minutes.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, until the vegetables are tender, about 20-25 minutes.
Stir in green beans and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
